text: Hey, I'm Andre, co-founder and CEO at Console.
We just launched our most popular feature yet, Console Assistant.
Now working in Console is like talking to another member of your team who always has
great ideas.
We think that using Console should be as easy as telling Console what you want it to do.
With Assistant, you can build, investigate, and handle tier two tasks all in natural language.
Let's take a look at what it can do.
Last week, we hired eight new employees.
Normally, onboarding them would take hours.
Today, we can automate it entirely with Assistant.
This is Assistant in action.
I just tell it what I need, answer a few clarifying questions, and it gets to work.
Here, it's building me an onboarding playbook that handles getting all new employees up
and running with Okta, Google, and Slack.
It works with you to understand exactly what you need to do, pulls in the relevant tools,
and generates the playbook for you.
So you'll never have to build a manual workflow again.
Hey, Sandeel.
How's your first week been?
Great.
But I have been trying to see if anyone's laptop has been compromised since I got here.
Can Console do that?
Of course.
We'll just ask Assistant.
Assistant can check CrowdStrike and see if any malware ran on employee devices in the
last 30 days.
You can just prompt it, and then Assistant gets to work.
It reaches into connected systems, pulls what it needs, and then acts on what it finds.
Unlike legacy products, Console doesn't rely on pre-built connectors.
It knows how to read API docs and build the appropriate connectors on its own.
It runs its own tests to make sure everything is working, and then it runs the action.
Assistant can handle Tier 2 work just like this in real time.
All right.
Looks like it found two suspicious devices.
Let's send them a DM and then lock their device the next 5 minutes.
Oh, great.
What do let's think to worry about?
Assistant is accessible from anywhere in Console.
For example, I just got a ticket that someone spilled water on their laptop.
And it's perfect for that kind of work.
Let's delegate it.
We've already got Console connected to CDW, so I can just tell it to order a new laptop
without having to leave the ticket view.
And just like that, new laptop's on its way.
We built Assistant because this is how people expect to work today.
Engineering teams figured it out first.
They stopped writing code and started delegating to agents.
We bring that same leverage to IT.
Console knows your systems, your policies, and the full context of your organization.
So when Tier 2 work lands in your queue, you don't have to route it, build a workflow,
or jump between tools.
Tell it what needs to happen, and it happens.
And that's Console Assistant.
